questions.text: The lower two pairs of ribs in humans are called
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option because lower ribs are called floating ribs.
It is not correct option because lower ribs are called floating ribs.

The lower two pairs of ribs in humans are called "floating ribs." These ribs do not connect directly to the sternum (breastbone) or to other ribs but are attached to the vertebral column in the back. They are also known as "vertebral ribs" or "false ribs."
It is not correct option because lower ribs are called floating ribs.

questions.text: The neurula is the stage in which embryo has
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option. It forms during blastula stage.
It is not correct option. Germ layers form in gastrulation.

The neurula stage is a developmental stage in embryonic development during which the embryo forms the neural tube, which will eventually develop into the brain and spinal cord. At this stage, the embryo is characterized by the presence of the neural plate, which folds and fuses to form the neural tube.
It is not correct option. It also develops in gastrulation stage.

questions.text: If the alterations involve only one or a few base pairs in the coding sequence they are called
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option because it is not only mutation but involves point mutation.

If alterations involve only one or a few base pairs in the coding sequence, they are typically referred to as "point mutations." These mutations involve a change in a single nucleotide base pair within the DNA sequence. Point mutations can have various effects on the resulting protein, ranging from no change to a minor alteration in the amino acid sequence to a significant change that affects protein function.
It is not correct option because deletion means removal of complete gene.
It is not correct option because interphase is related to cell divisions.

These mutations involve a change in a single nucleotide base pair within the DNA sequence. Point mutations can have various effects on the resulting protein, ranging from no change to a minor alteration in the amino acid sequence to a significant change that affects protein function.

questions.text: The period of life cycle of a cell between two consecutive divisions is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option because prophase is one of the phases of cell division.
It is not correct option because telophase is related to phases of cell division.
It is not correct option because the period is known as interphase.

The period of the cell cycle between two consecutive divisions is known as interphase. Interphase is the longest phase of the cell cycle and is divided into three sub phases. During interphase, the cell grows, replicates its DNA, and prepares for cell division. After interphase, the cell enters the mitotic phase.

questions.explanation: The period of the cell cycle between two consecutive divisions is known as interphase. Interphase is the longest phase of the cell cycle and is divided into three sub phases. During interphase, the cell grows, replicates its DNA, and prepares for cell division. After interphase, the cell enters the mitotic phase.

questions.text: During meiosis, the tetrad is formed in
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Leptotene is the initial stage of prophase I in meiosis. During leptotene, the chromosomes begin to condense. so it is not correct option.

Tetrad formation occurs during the zygotene stage of prophase I in meiosis. In zygotene, homologous chromosomes pair up to form structures known as bivalents or tetrads. Each tetrad consists of two homologous chromosomes, with each chromosome composed of two sister chromatids.
During pachytene, the homologous chromosomes, which have already paired up in zygotene, become fully aligned and tightly associated along their lengths. So it is not correct option.
The diplotene stage is a later substage of prophase I in meiosis. It is not correct option because in it tetrad begins to dissolve.

questions.explanation: Tetrad formation occurs during the zygotene stage of prophase I in meiosis. In zygotene, homologous chromosomes pair up to form structures known as bivalents or tetrads. Each tetrad consists of two homologous chromosomes, with each chromosome composed of two sister chromatids.

questions.text: The maturity on-set diabetes of the young(MODY) is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Maturity-onset diabetes of the young (MODY) is a group of monogenic forms of diabetes characterized by autosomal dominant inheritance and typically presents before the age of 25. About 50% of cases of MODY are caused by mutations in glucokinase gene.

It is not correct option because MODY is autosomal disease not sex linked.

questions.text: The gene pool consists of all alleles at all gene loci in all individuals of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option because individual is the basic component of population.
It is not correct option because specie means individuals with same characteristics.

The gene pool consists of all alleles at all gene loci in all individuals of a population. It represents the total genetic diversity within a population or species. This includes all the different versions (alleles) of genes present for each trait within the population.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option. Gypsies term has nothing to do with ecosystem.

swarmers may play a role in nutrient cycling, decomposition, or other ecological processes. They can rapidly colonize new habitats or exploit available resources. But they are not hardy invaders.

In ecological succession, pioneers are the first organisms to colonize a barren or disturbed habitat. These organisms are typically hardy and able to thrive in harsh conditions. As pioneers establish themselves, they pave the way for more complex and diverse species to inhabit the area over time.

questions.subject: Biology

The study of human populations is called demography. Demography involves the analysis of population size, structure, distribution, and dynamics, including factors such as birth rates, death rates, migration patterns, age distribution, fertility rates, and mortality rates.

questions.text: The homeostatic thermostat present in a brain part is called
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Thalamus is not involved in homeostasis.

The homeostatic thermostat present in a part of the brain is commonly referred to as the hypothalamus. The hypothalamus plays a crucial role in regulating various bodily functions, including temperature control, by receiving input from temperature receptors in the body and initiating responses to maintain internal stability or homeostasis.
Hippocampus is not related to homeostasis.
Amygdala is not related to homeostasis.

questions.text: The inflammatory or degenerative disease that damages joints is called
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The inflammatory or degenerative disease that damages joints is commonly referred to as arthritis. There are several types of arthritis, including osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and psoriatic arthritis, among others. These conditions can cause pain, stiffness, swelling, and reduced range of motion in affected joints, and they can have a significant impact on a person's quality of life.
Osteoporosis is not a joint-related disease but rather a condition characterized by weakening of the bones. so not a correct option.
Meningitis is an inflammatory disease characterized by the inflammation of the protective membranes covering the brain and spinal cord, known as the meninges. So not a correct option.
Spondylosis, also known as spinal osteoarthritis, is a condition characterized by the degeneration of the spine. so its not the best option.

questions.text: The leaf unrolling is promoted by red light in
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Bryophytes, such as mosses and liverworts, are non-vascular plants that lack true leaves, stems, and roots, so they do not undergo leaf unrolling in the same way as vascular plants.
They also do not show leaf unrolling except few species.
Dicots, another type of flowering plant, typically have seeds with two cotyledons do not show leaf unrolling.

Leaf unrolling is a process primarily observed in monocots, which are a type of flowering plant characterized by having seeds with a single cotyledon or seed leaf. Monocots include grasses, lilies, and palms.
